page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation, 8 physical cores, and 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results show average multi-core capabilities alongside weaker single-core performance. The stress-ng single-core test scores 1093.09, which sits in the bottom 25%, while the multi-core test reaches 8030.46, placing it in the middle 50%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are relatively weak, with gzip compression scoring 18.88 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression scoring 6.65 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth remains stable, with read-only small block cached speeds at 603.07 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 45.41 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this instance offers a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU threads. It performs adequately in static web serving at 7.92 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 8.63 million operations per second. However, it is less suited for memory-intensive databases or complex LLM inference, as demonstrated by a weak PassMark database score of 2040.05 and a poor 7B model text generation speed of 5.04 tokens per second. It is best utilized for general-purpose utility tasks, basic web serving, and lightweight multi-threaded processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.2xlarge is an x86_64 server configured with 8 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core processing and memory bandwidth, balanced by weaker single-core execution and database operations. Specifically, the instance achieves a stress-ng multi-core score of 8030.46 and a static web serving throughput of 7.92 GB/sec, though single-threaded compression and database benchmarks fall into lower percentiles. This hardware profile represents a cost-efficient option for non-critical, shared-resource environments. It is best suited for lightweight multi-threaded applications, basic web serving, and general utility workloads that do not demand sustained, dedicated CPU performance.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.2xlarge is an x86_64 server with 8 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline. It is designed for cost-effective, non-dedicated workloads like basic web serving.

tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-resource server featuring eight x86_64 cores and a balanced memory profile for basic web serving and utility workloads.
